mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates rapidly, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during high-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below -0.5 for long-short portfolios constructed on this signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts equity valuations over a one-month horizon specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism produces negative alpha when tested against a market-neutral benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes above 0.5 percent force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% threshold, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year windows, or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue forecasts and equity risk premium compression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 across all tested rolling windows since 1990.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below the 2017 baseline index of 100, manufacturing contraction signals imminent earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations where INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for market beta.
